questions.question: Explain the syntax and procedure to declare an array of 10 objects of a class Student with proper initialization using a parameterized constructor.
keyPoints: Syntax: Student s[10]; or with initialization Student s[3] = {Student(a,b), Student(c,d), Student(e,f)}
Default constructor called if no args, parameterized constructor needs values
Example: Student s[3] = {1,2,3}; where constructor accepts int
If parameterized constructor exists without default, all elements must be initialized

questions.question: How is memory allocated for an array of objects? Differentiate between stack and heap allocation.
keyPoints: Stack allocation: Student s[5]; - automatic memory, auto cleanup
Heap allocation: Student* s = new Student[5]; - manual memory, needs delete[]
Stack: faster access, limited size, auto deallocation
Heap: larger memory available, explicit deallocation required
Each object size × number of objects = total memory required

questions.question: Write a C++ program to create an array of 5 objects of a class containing roll number and name. Initialize using constructor and display all records.
keyPoints: Define class with private members: roll, name
Define constructor with parameters
Create array: Student s[5] with appropriate initialization
Use loop to access each object: s[i].display()
Display function should print roll number and name

questions.question: Explain how member functions are accessed for objects stored in an array with an example.
keyPoints: Use subscript operator [] to select object, then dot operator . for member
Syntax: arrayName[index].memberFunction()
Example: students[0].display(); calls display() for first student
Can use loop: for(i=0; i<n; i++) arr[i].func();
For pointers: use arrow operator -> or dereference first

questions.question: What is the role of destructors in arrays of objects? When are they invoked?
keyPoints: Destructor called once for each object in array
Invoked when array goes out of scope (stack) or delete[] is called (heap)
Called in reverse order of construction
Useful for releasing resources like memory, file handles
Example: ~Student() cleans up any dynamic memory in each object

questions.question: Differentiate between array of objects and array of pointers to objects with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Array of objects: Student s[5]; - stores actual objects
Array of pointers: Student* s[5]; - stores addresses of objects
Pointers allow polymorphism: basePtr[i] = new Derived();
Pointers need individual object creation with new
Objects: contiguous memory, simpler, no polymorphism
Pointers: flexible, dynamic, requires manual memory management

questions.question: How do you pass an array of objects to a function? Explain with code example.
keyPoints: Pass by reference: void display(Student (&s)[5])
Pass array with size: void func(Student arr[], int n)
Reference avoids copying entire array of objects
Example: void show(Student& s) { s.display(); }
Can also pass pointer: void func(Student* ptr)

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program to manage records of N employees using an array of objects. Include features to accept data, display all records, and search for an employee by ID.
keyPoints: Define Employee class with id, name, salary
Constructor for initialization
getter/setter methods
Array: Employee emp[N]; using parameterized constructor
accept() method to input employee details
display() method to show employee info
search() function to find by id
Main function demonstrating all operations
Proper use of loops and conditional statements
Handle edge cases like employee not found

questions.question: Explain the concept of dynamic allocation of arrays of objects in C++. Write a program to create, initialize, and delete an array of 10 objects dynamically.
keyPoints: Dynamic allocation using new keyword
Syntax: ClassName* ptr = new ClassName[size];
Need default or parameterized constructor
Access members: ptr[i].member or (ptr+i)->member
Must use delete[] to deallocate
Example with Student class: create, initialize with loop, display, delete
Memory leak prevention explanation
Comparison with stack allocation

questions.question: Design a class Matrix to represent a 3x3 matrix using array of objects where each object represents a row (array of 3 integers). Implement functions to input, display, and add two matrices.
keyPoints: Define class Matrix with array of 3 integers (one row)
Array of 3 Matrix objects = 3x3 matrix
Constructor for initialization
input() and display() functions
add() function taking another Matrix object and returning result matrix
Main: Matrix m1[3], m2[3], m3[3];
Loop through array to input/output each row
Demonstrate matrix addition

questions.question: Write a C++ program to create an array of objects of a class Circle to store radius and calculate area and circumference. Implement functions to find the circle with maximum area.
keyPoints: Define Circle class with radius data member
Constructor for initialization
Functions: area() returns πr², circumference() returns 2πr
Create array of Circle objects: Circle c[5];
Initialize with different radii
Function to find maximum area: compare c[i].area()
Display details of circle with max area
Use appropriate loops and comparison logic
Handle floating point output properly
